description: The access-log-exporter dashboard provides real-time visualization and analysis of web server access logs collected via syslog. It displays key HTTP metrics such as request rates, response times, status codes, and data transfer volumes, supporting both Nginx and Apache servers. The dashboard enables users to monitor traffic patterns, detect anomalies, and gain insights into application performance and reliability, making it ideal for operational monitoring and troubleshooting. Metrics are customizable through presets, allowing tailored views for different monitoring needs.

panels.description:
The breakdown of the various HTTP status codes of the requests handled within' this period that matches the variables chosen above.

This chart helps notice and dive into which service is having failures and of what kind.

panels.description:
This graph can help assess and help us meet SLA requirements as far as the responsive time of our services.

For a more detailed latency graph broken out by ingress please open the closed tab at the bottom because it is very CPU intensive.
panels.title: Latency (Average Percentiles)

panels.description: This panel indicates the current status of the NGINX server for the selected instance, showing whether it's up or down.
panels.title: NGINX Status

panels.description: This panel tracks the number of processed connections for the selected instance, displaying both active and handled connections to monitor server activity.
panels.title: Processed connections

panels.description: This panel shows NGINX active connections for the selected instance, categorized as writing, waiting, reading, and total active.
panels.title: Active Connections
